Candidate Evaluation Report: Elementary School Principal Position

Name Education Work Experience Notes
Adrienne Kowalski B.A. Elementary Education, University of Michigan, 2014. M.Ed. Educational Leadership, University of Michigan, 2016. Graduated with distinction; capstone project examined how structured morning meeting routines affect classroom community and early literacy outcomes in grades two and three. Second and third grade classroom teacher, Riverside Elementary School (2014 - 2018): developed a schoolwide read-aloud calendar adopted by all primary grades and led a voluntary after-school tutoring group each semester. Assistant Principal, Riverside Elementary School (2018 - 2022): managed daily building operations, coordinated family engagement events, and supervised a staff of thirty-four teachers and paraprofessionals. Acting Principal, Riverside Elementary School (2022 - present): assumed full leadership responsibilities following the retirement of the previous principal; oversaw two consecutive accreditation reviews with no findings requiring corrective action. Strong communication skills noted by the full panel. Expressed genuine interest in mentoring early-career teachers. Salary expectation is slightly above the posted band but may be negotiable given her depth of site-level leadership experience.
Marcus Delgado B.A. Child Development, University of Texas at Austin, 2011. Fourth and fifth grade teacher, Cedarwood Elementary School (2011 - 2017): consistently received strong classroom observation ratings and was selected twice by colleagues to facilitate professional development workshops on differentiated instruction. Curriculum Coordinator, Cedarwood Elementary School (2017 - 2021): aligned the school's language arts and mathematics scope and sequence documents to updated state standards, working closely with grade-level teams over two full academic years. Instructional Coach, Westfield Elementary School (2021 - present): provides weekly coaching cycles to classroom teachers, conducts demonstration lessons, and leads collaborative planning sessions for all grade bands.
Priya Nambiar B.Ed. Primary Education, University of Delhi, 2013. M.Ed. Curriculum and Instruction, Georgia State University, 2015. Currently enrolled in a doctoral program in Educational Leadership at Georgia State University; completed all coursework and comprehensive examinations and is in the dissertation writing stage. Withdrew temporarily from the program in 2017 to accept a full-time school leadership role but re-enrolled in 2021. Referred by current assistant principal Tomas Ferreira. Panel flagged a potential concern about expectations around schedule flexibility, as the candidate indicated she would need protected time on certain evenings for dissertation work. Recommend a follow-up conversation to clarify boundaries before advancing.
Jordan Whitfield Kindergarten and first grade teacher, Maplewood Elementary School (2009 - 2016): built a classroom environment widely recognized within the building for its calm tone and high student engagement; mentored three student teachers from the local university over that period. Dean of Students, Maplewood Elementary School (2016 - 2021): managed attendance, family communication, and behavioral support for a student population of roughly four hundred and fifty, and helped redesign the school's approach to restorative conversations following disciplinary incidents. Assistant Principal, Harborview Elementary School (2021 - present): shares oversight of curriculum, staffing, and community partnerships with the principal, and leads the school's family advisory council meetings each month. Holds no formal graduate degree but has completed substantial graduate coursework in school administration across two institutions without finishing a program. Panel unanimously agreed that her breadth and quality of hands-on leadership experience more than compensates, and that the absence of a completed degree should not be disqualifying given the strength of her references and her record of results.
Svetlana Obrekov B.A. Pedagogy and Early Childhood Education, Sofia University, 2009. M.Ed. Educational Administration, Pennsylvania State University, 2012. First through third grade teacher, Crestwood Elementary School (2012 - 2018): introduced a structured approach to small-group reading instruction that the school later adopted across all primary grades, and served for three years as the building representative to the district curriculum committee. Assistant Principal, Crestwood Elementary School (2018 - 2023): supervised daily operations, managed the master schedule, coordinated all state testing logistics, and oversaw hiring for twelve open teaching positions across five years. Senior Instructional Leader, Eastbrook Elementary School (2023 - 2024): a newly created role designed to bridge curriculum planning and building administration; the position was eliminated following a district budget reduction in early 2024, and she has been seeking a permanent principal role since that time. Available to start immediately. Open to a full in-person schedule. Brings considerable depth in early literacy instructional design and has lighter experience in upper elementary grades, but expressed confidence in her ability to build credibility with those grade-level teams given time.
Darnell Osei B.S. Elementary Education, Howard University, 2020. Third grade teacher, Pinecrest Elementary School (2020 - 2023): supported students through two interrupted school years and earned recognition from building leadership for maintaining strong family communication during that period. Lead Teacher, Pinecrest Elementary School (2023 - present): coordinates grade-level planning meetings, assists with new teacher onboarding, and serves as a point of contact for families when the assistant principal is unavailable.
Camille Tran B.A. Psychology, University of California Los Angeles, 2008. M.Ed. School Counseling, Johns Hopkins University, 2011. Completed additional graduate coursework in building administration and supervision through two summer institutes offered by the state department of education between 2019 and 2021. School Counselor, Sunnyside Elementary School (2011 - 2019): provided individual and small-group counseling, coordinated referrals to outside support services, and facilitated grade-level lessons on social and emotional skills twice per month. Assistant Principal, Sunnyside Elementary School (2019 - present): leads the school's family engagement work, manages all student support team meetings, and co-facilitates weekly check-ins with classroom teachers to monitor student progress and wellbeing.
Reuben Glasscock Application was incomplete at the submission deadline. Packet included only a brief cover letter; no resume, letters of reference, or administrative credential documentation were attached. Retained in the system per human resources policy but not recommended for an interview at this time.
Fatima Al-Rashidi B.A. Early Childhood Education, American University of Beirut, 2016. M.Ed. Educational Leadership, Technical University of Munich, 2019. Kindergarten teacher, Greenfield International School (2019 - 2022): taught in a multilingual classroom setting, developed family communication materials in three languages, and was selected by the head of school to serve on a cross-campus committee reviewing early childhood teaching practices. Assistant Principal, Fairview Elementary School (2022 - present): manages day-to-day building operations, leads new staff orientation each fall, and coordinates closely with the school social worker and family liaison on support plans for students experiencing significant challenges at home. Brings a strong background in multilingual learning environments that would serve our student population well. Panel would like to see her lead a full staff meeting or professional development session before making a final decision, as her experience presenting to large adult audiences is less documented than her other qualifications. Recommend a second-round visit with a structured presentation component.
Oliver Hutchins B.A. Primary Education, Oxford Brookes University, 2005. Postgraduate Certificate in Educational Leadership, University of London, 2007. Year three and four classroom teacher, Elmfield Church of England Primary School, London (2007 - 2012): served on the school's behavior and attendance committee and coordinated the annual whole-school arts week each spring. Deputy Headteacher, St. Barnabas Primary School, London (2012 - 2020): shared responsibility for curriculum planning, staff performance reviews, and communication with governors; led the school through two external inspections, both with favorable outcomes. Headteacher, Thornton Park Primary School, London (2020 - present): oversees all aspects of school leadership for a two-form entry primary school of approximately three hundred and sixty pupils. Relocating from London; work authorization is in progress. Timeline estimated at eight to twelve weeks. Strong candidate overall. Worth holding the position if the hiring timeline is acceptable to the superintendent.
